fact: She has been carrying unsaid words of love for Pace and has a self-imposed rule that she will not say 'I love you' before he does

fact: She said her cracker-and-meal sentence aloud to Pace — the full prepared sentence, whole, as she had built it

fact: Pace responded to her sentence with physical warmth: a deliberate pull closer, a longer-than-usual held kiss on her crown, his cheek settling against her hair

fact: Something in Pace's response did not match the shape of what she had said — her body registered a mismatch before her mind could name it

fact: She will not say the cracker-and-meal sentence again; the saying was a one-time opening and Pace did not give the answer she was waiting for

fact: Pace's sentence and her sentence were 'not the same shape'

fact: She was received warmly

fact: She does not know what happened in the exchange — whether Pace understood her and chose to respond as he did, or did not understand

fact: The smell of his flannel shirt she had been sleeping in at home was only an approximate, stored version of his actual scent; the real thing is warmer and more specific than she had understood

fact: He has a surgical scar on the inside of his thigh, close to the hip, old and smooth and slightly raised
